_README: State-level NEC adoption status as of 2026-Q1. Source: NFPA NEC adoption tracker + state electrical-board websites (https://www.nfpa.org/News-and-Research/Publications-and-media/NFPA-Journal/2024/NEC-Adoption-Map). Many AHJ enforce a LATER edition than the state default — always confirm with the local building dept. Values are the edition number string the wizard / engine accepts: '2023' | '2020' | '2017'.

AL.note: Adopted statewide 2023; some municipalities still on 2017.

AZ.note: No statewide adoption — AHJ-by-AHJ. Phoenix and Tucson on 2017; Scottsdale 2020. Confirm locally.

CA.note: California Electrical Code Title 24 Part 3 — re-publishes NEC every 3y. CEC 2022 = NEC 2020 + amendments; CEC 2025 = NEC 2023 + amendments (effective 2026-01-01).

CO.note: Statewide adoption via state electrical board; effective 2023-09-01.

FL.note: Florida Building Code 8th ed (2023) references NEC 2023; effective 2024-01-01.

HI.note: State on 2017; Honolulu County uses Hawaii Electrical Code (HEC) = NEC 2020 + Hawaii Rule 14H interconnection amendments. Confirm island/county.

IL.note: No statewide — Chicago has its own electrical code (CEC) loosely tracking NEC.

MA.note: Massachusetts Electrical Code 527 CMR 12 = NEC 2023 + state amendments; effective 2023-08-25.

MI.note: Statewide on 2017 — adoption cycles run 6y+ behind. Major cities sometimes on 2020.

NY.note: NY State Uniform Code refs 2020; NYC has its own electrical code (NYCEC) loosely tracking NEC.

OH.note: Ohio Board of Building Standards on 2017 — adoption pending.

PA.note: No statewide — UCC refs 2017. Many cities ahead.

TX.note: No statewide — adopted municipality-by-municipality. Austin 2020; Dallas 2020; Houston 2020; Lewisville 2020. Confirm AHJ.

WI.note: Statewide on 2017 — adoption lag is well-known.
